name: Respenda de la Peña from Guardo
name_original: Respenda de la Peña desde Guardo – circular por el río Villavega y Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
description: A difficult 74.4-mile road cycling route from Guardo, gaining 3183 feet through the stunning Parque Natural Montaña Palentina.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Guardo navigate the diverse terrain of the Montaña Palentina region in Palencia, Spain. The area is characterized by its mountainous landscape, with elevations ranging from river valleys to the slopes of the Sierra del Brezo. Cyclists encounter varied topography, including significant elevation changes and scenic roads alongside the Carrión river and reservoirs. This setting provides a robust environment for road cycling, combining challenging climbs with more gentle rides. Best road cycling routes around Guardo The most popular road cycling route is Montaña Palentina: Cervera and Guardo Loop, a 61.9 miles (99.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views across the Montaña Palentina. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Guardo and Velilla del Río Carrión from Puente Almuhey – circular via Siero de la Reina, a difficult 41.8 miles (67.3 km) path. This circular route connects towns via scenic roads, often following river valleys. Local road cyclists also love the Saldaña from Guardo – circular via Resp, a 57.8 miles (93.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Guardo is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and scenic roads alongside reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Guardo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Guardo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Guardo?
A: There are 15 road cycling routes around Guardo listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Guardo?
A: Guardo is situated in the Montaña Palentina region, characterized by its mountainous landscape. You can expect significant elevation changes, with routes traversing river valleys, the southern slopes of the Sierra del Brezo, and offering views of higher peaks like Espigüete. The terrain includes an impressive landscape of needles, cliffs, ravines, and deep valleys, providing both challenging climbs and scenic rides.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Guardo?
A: Yes, Guardo offers several challenging routes. 10 of the 15 routes listed on komoot are classified as 'difficult'. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154644602/montana-palentina-cervera-and-guardo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montaña Palentina: Cervera and Guardo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 61.9-mile (99.7 km) route with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, offering extensive views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6972453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panderrueda Pass – View of the Picos de Europa loop from Guardo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 144 km with over 2,200 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, 5 of the 15 routes around Guardo are rated as 'moderate'. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154588183/saldana-from-guardo-circular-via-resp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldaña from Guardo – circular via Resp&lt;/a&gt;, a 57.8-mile (93.1 km) trail that leads through varied landscapes and can be completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Guardo often pass by stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can ride alongside the Carrión river, through the Montaña Palentina Natural Park with its diverse forests, and enjoy panoramic views from various viewpoints. Highlights in the area include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5200388" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roblón of El Monasterio&lt;/a&gt;, the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5793535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velilla del Río Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5649404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Brezo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Guardo?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042296134/guardo-and-velilla-del-rio-carrion-from-puente-almuhey-circular-via-siero-de-la-reina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo and Velilla del Río Carrión from Puente Almuhey – circular via Siero de la Reina&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 41.8-mile (67.3 km) path that connects towns via scenic roads, often following river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154588183/saldana-from-guardo-circular-via-resp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saldaña from Guardo – circular via Resp&lt;/a&gt; is another great circular option.

Q: Can I find routes that follow the 'Ruta de los Pantanos' (Reservoirs Route)?
A: Yes, the 'Ruta de los Pantanos' is easily accessible from Guardo. Velilla del Río Carrión, just 5 kilometers upstream, serves as the starting point for this scenic route. It follows the P-210 road alongside the Compuerto and Camporredondo reservoirs, offering continuous scenic riding through mountainous landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Guardo?
A: The Montaña Palentina region, where Guardo is located, offers varied conditions. While specific seasonal advice isn't provided, the presence of high mountains suggests that spring, summer, and early autumn would generally be the most favorable times for road cycling, avoiding potential snow or harsh winter conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that are part of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Guardo is part of the 'Camino Olvidado a Santiago,' or the 'Forgotten Way' of St. James. This historic pilgrimage route offers unique cycling opportunities, allowing riders to follow paths with centuries of history through picturesque scenery. While not exclusively road cycling routes, many sections are suitable for road bikes.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints for cyclists in the Montaña Palentina region?
A: The Montaña Palentina region boasts numerous viewpoints offering panoramic vistas. Cyclists can enjoy impressive views from locations such as Piedrasluengas, Alto de la Varga, Alba, and Alto de Peñas Negras, which are often accessible via scenic secondary roads.

Q: What kind of traffic can I expect on the roads around Guardo?
A: The Montaña Palentina region is recognized for its secondary roads experiencing light traffic, making it an appealing destination for road cyclists seeking serene rides through stunning landscapes and charming villages.
tips: Guardo is a municipality in the province of Palencia, Castilla y León, on the banks of the Carrión river. The town is the gateway to the Montaña Palentina Natural Park which attracts many adventure tourists to the area.

Town of the Palencia Mountain whose name seems to be that it comes from "steep or pindian place".

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging racebike route, covering nearly 120 km, usually takes around 5 hours to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as difficult and features a mountainous landscape with significant elevation gains and demanding road passes. While many sections are well-paved, be prepared for potentially unpaved and difficult-to-ride segments, especially on steeper climbs. Very good fitness is required, and you may need to push your bike at times.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or casual cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this is a demanding route designed for experienced road cyclists with very good fitness. Its significant elevation gains and potentially unpaved sections make it unsuitable for beginners or those seeking a casual ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can anticipate stunning natural beauty typical of the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. The area features lush forests, picturesque reservoirs, and imposing steep peaks, offering a captivating experience through a relatively unspoiled natural environment. The route also follows the río Villavega, providing riverine landscapes.

faqs.question: Where does this cycling route start and end?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route that starts and ends in Guardo. You can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months when the weather is more stable and favorable for cycling in mountainous terrain. Avoid winter, as conditions can be harsh and some sections may be impassable due to snow or ice.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: There are no specific cycling permits or entrance fees required to access the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina for cycling. However, as it is a protected natural area, always adhere to general park regulations regarding environmental protection and respectful use.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, it is primarily a demanding road cycling route. Bringing dogs on such a long and challenging ride, especially with potential unpaved sections and traffic, is generally not recommended for their safety and comfort. If you do bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, can keep pace, and is always on a leash when appropriate.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route coincide with other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino Olvidado (Matamorisca-Puente Almuhey) for about 20% of the journey, and briefly on the Camino Natural del Románico Palentino. Ruta del Carbón de cok. Etapa 2 for 2%.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or towns along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the starting point of Guardo, the route passes through the settlement of Respenda de la Peña. The entire journey is set within the stunning Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, offering continuous natural beauty.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's crucial to bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained racebike suitable for varied terrain, plenty of water and snacks, repair kit, first-aid supplies, navigation tools, and weather-appropriate clothing. Due to the demanding nature, consider carrying extra layers and emergency supplies.
